title: P1/P2 Critical Issue Alerting
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:issue`. from the `issue` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/issues`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per active issue per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `priority` (string), `category` (string), `name` (string), `deviceName` (string), `status` (string), `issueId` (string), `siteId` (string), `lastOccurenceTime` (epoch milliseconds).
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" (priority="P1" OR priority="P2") status!="RESOLVED" | stats count as open_issues values(name) as issue_names values(category) as categories by priority | sort priority
description: Alerts on unresolved P1 and P2 priority issues from Catalyst Center Assurance, which represent critical and high-impact network problems requiring immediate attention.
value: P1 and P2 issues affect large numbers of users or critical infrastructure. Immediate alerting in Splunk ensures they are routed to the right team within minutes.
implementation: Enable the `issue` input in the Cisco Catalyst TA. Confirm `status` and `priority` field extractions match Catalyst Center (for example `RESOLVED` for closed issues). Schedule this as an alert with short cadence for P1/P2 backlogs.
visualization: Single value or table (open_issues by priority), list of issue names and categories for the alert body.

references.title: Catalyst Center Issues API ‚Äî Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Planned maintenance generating P1/P2 issues for devices undergoing reload.** Devices being upgraded fire connectivity and health issues at P1/P2 priority. Distinguish by correlating with ITSM change records or `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"` for approved maintenance activity on the affected devices. Suppress by using a `catalyst_maintenance_windows` lookup keyed on `deviceName` and time range, and suppressing P1/P2 alerts for devices within their maintenance window.

**Assurance auto-resolution clearing issues within minutes of detection.** Some P1/P2 issues are transient: Assurance detects and resolves them within 5-10 minutes (e.g., brief device unreachability during STP reconvergence). Distinguish by checking whether `status` transitions to RESOLVED within the same poll cycle. Suppress by requiring P1/P2 issues to persist with `status=ACTIVE` across 2+ consecutive polls before paging operations.

**Known Catalyst Center bug generating spurious P1/P2 issues.** Certain Catalyst Center versions have bugs that generate false P1/P2 issues (e.g., device health threshold miscalculations). Distinguish by checking the Cisco bug search for the specific `name` pattern. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_known_bugs` lookup and filtering issues that match known-spurious patterns.

**P1/P2 issue on a lab or non-production device.** A lab device or proof-of-concept equipment may generate P1/P2 issues that do not warrant operational paging. Distinguish by checking whether `deviceName` matches a lab naming convention or is assigned to a non-production `siteId`. Suppress by maintaining a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup for lab equipment and filtering them from P1/P2 alerting.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ The **issue** modular input is enabled; raw JSON exposes `priority`, `status`, `name`, and `category` with the same spelling and case Catalyst Center returns.
‚Ä¢ Confirm the **closed** status token (commonly `RESOLVED`) in your tenant‚Äîa mismatch causes endless pages or no pages at all.
‚Ä¢ On-call runbook: who owns **P1** vs **P2** and when to open a **Cisco TAC** high-severity case in parallel.
‚Ä¢ RBAC: **`SUPER-ADMIN-ROLE`** or **`NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE`** (or the tenant equivalent with read access to all active issues). In multi-tenant MSP deployments, align the input scope to the org you page for.
‚Ä¢ `docs/implementation-guide.md` for credential rotation and audit logging on the host that runs the modular input.

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
‚Ä¢ **Intent API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/issues` with the **active/open** filter set the add-on supports (read the **TA README** for ‚Äúopen only‚Äù and default query parameters).
‚Ä¢ **TA input name:** **issue**; sourcetype `cisco:dnac:issue`, index `catalyst` (or your chosen index; update the SPL to match).
‚Ä¢ **Interval:** **300 seconds (5 minutes)** is typical for fresher P1/P2 backlogs. If the TA polls every 5m but Splunk runs this alert every 15m, that is a deliberate 10‚Äì12 minute maximum lag‚Äîdocument it for on-call. Align poll interval, Splunk schedule, and MTTD expectations together.
‚Ä¢ **Volume:** the P1/P2 subset should be small in a healthy org; if `open_issues` is huge, the TA may be re-importing full history on every poll (see troubleshooting).
‚Ä¢ **Key fields for this alert:** `priority` (P1/P2), `status` (to exclude closed), `name`, `category`; add `issueId` to a v2 of the search for ITSM idempotency and deduplication.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" (priority="P1" OR priority="P2") status!="RESOLVED" | stats count as open_issues values(name) as issue_names values(category) as categories by priority | sort priority
```

Understanding this SPL (status strings, payload size, throttling)
‚Ä¢ **`status!="RESOLVED"`** follows Cisco‚Äôs default string; if your tenant returns `Resolved` or localized text, use `| eval s=upper(status) | where s!="RESOLVED"` in a macro and reference the macro in saved searches.
‚Ä¢ **P1/P2 only** by design‚Äîroute P3/P4 to a daily digest, not the pager.
‚Ä¢ **`values(name)`** in email can grow large‚Äîtruncate with `mvindex` in a `eval` for the email body, or use a ServiceNow/ITSM mid-tier to format the payload.
‚Ä¢ For chronic issues, add **`issueId`** in `stats ... by` and use alert suppression on `issueId` so the same TAC case does not re-page every 15 minutes after acknowledgement.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
‚Ä¢ The search filters to P1 and P2, excluding resolved issues.
‚Ä¢ `stats` counts open items per `priority` and rolls up `name` and `category` for context in the alert body.
‚Ä¢ `sort priority` is lexicographic; for strict P1-before-P2 ordering when labels ever change, use a numeric `sev` from `replace`+`tonumber` in a macro (typical P1‚ÄìP4 strings sort acceptably in most deployments).

Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
‚Ä¢ Create or use a test **P2** in a lab, leave it open, and confirm the search preview returns non-zero `open_issues` with sensible `issue_names` and `categories`.
‚Ä¢ On a one-off search, `| where status="RESOLVED"` on recent data to confirm the TA does mark resolution in Splunk; if nothing is ever RESOLVED, fix field extraction or props before relying on the alert filter.
‚Ä¢ `| timechart count by priority` for P1/P2 only: healthy orgs should see low sustained lines; a sky-high P2 count often means the TA is re-sending the whole backlog (dedup with `issueId` or fix the input).
‚Ä¢ Compare counts to **Catalyst Center > Issues** with active P1/P2 filters in the same 15‚Äì30 minute window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize (alerting)
‚Ä¢ **Schedule:** every **15 minutes** with time range **last 16 minutes** (1 minute overlap) or **last 15 minutes**; overlaps reduce missed edges when polls and the scheduler do not line up.
‚Ä¢ **Trigger:** number of results **> 0** (or refine with `open_issues>0` after a stats reshape for your org‚Äôs alert builder).
‚Ä¢ **Throttle / suppression:** recommend a global cap of one alert per 15 minutes per run, plus per-`issueId` or per-`priority` throttling of 30‚Äì60 minutes when NOC has acknowledged in ITSM. Use Splunk‚Äôs alert suppression features or a lookup of ‚Äúpaged in last hour‚Äù for the same `issueId` once you add it to the search.
‚Ä¢ **Actions:** P1 to primary on-call (SMS/voice), P2 to a collaboration channel; include `siteId`/`deviceId` if you extend the search. Deep-link to Catalyst **Issues** in the action template.
‚Ä¢ ‚ÄúRecovered‚Äù or ‚Äúall clear‚Äù webhooks are optional; many teams close lifecycle in ServiceNow only.

Step 5 ‚Äî Troubleshooting
‚Ä¢ **Pages never stop:** status string mismatch or the same `issueId` re-alerting; normalize status with `upper()` and add `issueId` to the pipeline for deduplication.
‚Ä¢ **No page on a known P1:** RBAC on the service account, wrong Catalyst URL or virtual domain, or the **issue** input is pointed at a controller that is not the one showing the P1 in the UI.
‚Ä¢ **Thousands of P2 results:** the input may re-pull the entire backlog every poll; enable an ‚Äúactive only‚Äù or equivalent filter in the add-on, or pre-dedup on `issueId` in a summary saved search the alert calls.
‚Ä¢ **Upgrades** that rename `name` or duplicate `issueId`: deduplicate in a sibling search and document the change in the runbook; re-test after every TA or Catalyst major release.
‚Ä¢ **No issue events arriving:** verify the `issue` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
‚Ä¢ **Missing fields or empty values:** some issue fields vary by Catalyst Center version; run `| fieldsummary` to identify NULL or zero values that need `fillnull`.
‚Ä¢ **Fewer events than the Catalyst Center UI shows:** the API returns only active issues by default; if credential access is denied, check the service account RBAC role.
grandmaExplanation: We immediately flag the most serious network problems ‚Äî the ones that could affect lots of people ‚Äî so your team can jump on them right away.
